text: The terminal ileum is exteriorized through the old ileostomy site, with white arrows indicating traction caused by interconnecting terminal ileal branches. Translumination reveals the vascular arcade within the terminal ileal mesentery. Macroscopic perfusion of the terminal ileum is assessed by clamping the interconnecting terminal ileal branches before ligation.

The surgeon ligates the arterial branches to obtain additional mesenteric length for the pouch, ensuring no perfusion anomalies during white light assessment. After pouch creation, a bolus of NDC green is injected, and fluorescence angiography reveals suboptimal perfusion at the pouch tip, which is reinforced with PDS-IV-Zero sutures.
